Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 70 upvotes, 6 comments
  2. Teaching Claude to QA a mobile app 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 295 upvotes, 60 comments
  4. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 129 upvotes, 24 comments
  5. We indexed the Delve audit leak: 533 reports, 455 companies, 99.8% identical 71 upvotes, 11 comments
  6. My first patch to the Linux kernel 202 upvotes, 43 comments
  7. How to Attract AI Bots to Your Open Source Project 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 26 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. What Young Workers Are Doing to AI-Proof Themselves 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. 25 Years of Eggs 220 upvotes, 62 comments
  11. Vectorization of Verilog Designs and its Effects on Verification and Synthesis 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Palantir extends reach into British state as gets access to sensitive FCA data 95 upvotes, 24 comments
  13. A review of dice that came with the white castle 113 upvotes, 33 comments
  14. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 261 upvotes, 92 comments
  15. GrapheneOS refuses to comply with new age verification laws for operating system 110 upvotes, 40 comments
  16. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 62 upvotes, 20 comments
  17. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 114 upvotes, 45 comments
  18. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 103 upvotes, 44 comments
  19. Learnings from training a font recognition model from scratch 23 upvotes, 6 comments
  20. PC Gamer Recommends RSS Readers in a 37MB Article That Just Keeps Downloading 95 upvotes, 45 comments
  21. The Future of Version Control 270 upvotes, 150 comments
  22. Personal Computing (2022) 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  23. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 327 upvotes, 237 comments
  24. OpenClaw Is a Security Nightmare Dressed Up as a Daydream 158 upvotes, 117 comments
  25. Why I love NixOS 118 upvotes, 100 comments
  26. Windows native app development is a mess 253 upvotes, 261 comments
  27. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 129 upvotes, 129 comments
  28. Turkish Coffee? Since the 16th Century, It's in the Water 12 upvotes, 6 comments
  29. A case against currying 79 upvotes, 99 comments
  30. Zero ZGC4: A Better Graphing Calculator for School and Beyond 19 upvotes, 20 comments

Last generated 5:00am, 23 March 2026. Source here.
